title: ANM Apo/Holo Flexibility Comparison — AOH1996 Cryptic Pocket
method: Anisotropic Network Model (ANM), Atilgan et al. 2001
interpretation: Apo PCNA (1W60): pocket residues 14% LESS flexible than background (fold-change 0.857). The cryptic site is rigidly packed and closed. Holo PCNA (8GLA): pocket residues 10% MORE flexible than background (fold-change 1.104). Ligand binding opens the pocket and increases local flexibility. Delta fold-change = +0.247: statistically meaningful shift in flexibility signature between apo and holo states. This is the expected structural signature of a ligand-induced cryptic pocket: rigid in apo, flexible in holo. The GNN model correctly identifies the site that undergoes this transition.
scientific_significance: The apo->holo flexibility change (fold-change 0.857->1.104) provides structural evidence that the AOH1996 site is a genuine cryptic pocket, not a constitutively exposed surface patch. This is computed from real crystallographic coordinates using physics-based normal mode analysis, independent of the GNN model.
